```go
package models

import (
	"time"

	"github.com/google/uuid"
)

// Employee represents an employee entity
// @Description Employee information
type Employee struct {
	ID         string     `json:"id"`
	FirstName  string     `json:"first_name" example:"John"`
	LastName   string     `json:"last_name" example:"Doe"`
	Email      string     `json:"email" example:"john.doe@company.com"`
	Department string     `json:"department" example:"Engineering"`
	Position   string     `json:"position" example:"Senior Developer"`
	Salary     float64    `json:"salary" example:"85000.00"`
	Phone      string     `json:"phone,omitempty" example:"+1-555-0123"`
	Address    string     `json:"address,omitempty" example:"123 Main St, City, ST 12345"`
	Active     bool       `json:"active" example:"true"`
	CreatedAt  time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
	UpdatedAt  time.Time  `json:"updated_at"`
	DeletedAt  *time.Time `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

// CreateEmployeeRequest represents the request body for creating an employee
// @Description Request body for creating a new employee
type CreateEmployeeRequest struct {
	FirstName  string  `json:"first_name" example:"John"`
	LastName   string  `json:"last_name" example:"Doe"`
	Email      string  `json:"email" example:"john.doe@company.com"`
	Department string  `json:"department" example:"Engineering"`
	Position   string  `json:"position" example:"Senior Developer"`
	Salary     float64 `json:"salary" example:"85000.00"`
	Phone      string  `json:"phone,omitempty" example:"+1-555-0123"`
	Address    string  `json:"address,omitempty" example:"123 Main St"`
}

// UpdateEmployeeRequest represents the request body for partial update (PATCH)
// @Description Request body for updating employee fields (all fields optional)
type UpdateEmployeeRequest struct {
	FirstName  *string  `json:"first_name,omitempty" example:"John"`
	LastName   *string  `json:"last_name,omitempty" example:"Doe"`
	Email      *string  `json:"email,omitempty" example:"john.doe@company.com"`
	Department *string  `json:"department,omitempty" example:"Engineering"`
	Position   *string  `json:"position,omitempty" example:"Senior Developer"`
	Salary     *float64 `json:"salary,omitempty" example:"90000.00"`
	Phone      *string  `json:"phone,omitempty" example:"+1-555-0123"`
	Address    *string  `json:"address,omitempty" example:"456 New St, City, ST 12345"`
	Active     *bool    `json:"active,omitempty" example:"true"`
}

// BulkDeleteRequest represents the request body for bulk deletion
// @Description Request body for deleting multiple employees
type BulkDeleteRequest struct {
	IDs []string `json:"ids" example:"[\"uuid-1\",\"uuid-2\",\"uuid-3\"]"`
}

// EmployeeListResponse represents paginated list response
// @Description Paginated list of employees
type EmployeeListResponse struct {
	Data       []Employee `json:"data"`
	Total      int64      `json:"total"`
	Page       int        `json:"page"`
	PageSize   int        `json:"page_size"`
	TotalPages int        `json:"total_pages"`
}

// ErrorResponse represents an error response
// @Description Error response details
type ErrorResponse struct {
	Error   string `json:"error" example:"Error message"`
	Message string `json:"message,omitempty" example:"Detailed error description"`
}

// SuccessResponse represents a success response
// @Description Generic success response
type SuccessResponse struct {
	Message string      `json:"message" example:"Operation successful"`
	Data    interface{} `json:"data,omitempty"`
}

// NewEmployee creates a new employee with generated ID and timestamps
func NewEmployee(req CreateEmployeeRequest) *Employee {
	now := time.Now()
	return &Employee{
		ID:         uuid.New().String(),
		FirstName:  req.FirstName,
		LastName:   req.LastName,
		Email:      req.Email,
		Department: req.Department,
		Position:   req.Position,
		Salary:     req.Salary,
		Phone:      req.Phone,
		Address:    req.Address,
		Active:     true,
		CreatedAt:  now,
		UpdatedAt:  now,
		DeletedAt:  nil,
	}
}
```

## 3. In-Memory Database (`database/store.go`)

```go
package database

import (
	"employee-service/models"
	"sync"
	"time"
)

// Store represents the in-memory data store
type Store struct {
	employees map[string]*models.Employee
	mu        sync.RWMutex
}

// Global instance
var DB *Store

// InitStore initializes the in-memory database
func InitStore() {
	DB = &Store{
		employees: make(map[string]*models.Employee),
	}
	seedData()
}

// seedData populates the store with sample data for read-heavy scenarios
func seedData() {
	sampleEmployees := []models.CreateEmployeeRequest{
		{FirstName: "Alice", LastName: "Johnson", Email: "alice.johnson@acme.com", Department: "Engineering", Position: "Senior Developer", Salary: 95000, Phone: "+1-555-0101", Address: "101 Tech Blvd, Silicon Valley, CA 94025"},
		{FirstName: "Bob", LastName: "Smith", Email: "bob.smith@acme.com", Department: "Engineering", Position: "Developer", Salary: 75000, Phone: "+1-555-0102", Address: "102 Code Lane, Austin, TX 78701"},
		{FirstName: "Carol", LastName: "Williams", Email: "carol.williams@acme.com", Department: "Marketing", Position: "Marketing Manager", Salary: 85000, Phone: "+1-555-0103", Address: "103 Market St, New York, NY 10001"},
		{FirstName: "David", LastName: "Brown", Email: "david.brown@acme.com", Department: "Engineering", Position: "Tech Lead", Salary: 120000, Phone: "+1-555-0104", Address: "104 Architect Ave, Seattle, WA 98101"},
		{FirstName: "Eva", LastName: "Davis", Email: "eva.davis@acme.com", Department: "HR", Position: "HR Specialist", Salary: 65000, Phone: "+1-555-0105", Address: "105 People Pl, Denver, CO 80201"},
		{FirstName: "Frank", LastName: "Miller", Email: "frank.miller@acme.com", Department: "Engineering", Position: "Junior Developer", Salary: 55000, Phone: "+1-555-0106", Address: "106 Entry Rd, Chicago, IL 60601"},
		{FirstName: "Grace", LastName: "Wilson", Email: "grace.wilson@acme.com", Department: "Finance", Position: "Financial Analyst", Salary: 80000, Phone: "+1-555-0107", Address: "107 Money Dr, Boston, MA 02101"},
		{FirstName: "Henry", LastName: "Taylor", Email: "henry.taylor@acme.com", Department: "Engineering", Position: "DevOps Engineer", Salary: 90000, Phone: "+1-555-0108", Address: "108 Cloud Ct, Portland, OR 97201"},
		{FirstName: "Ivy", LastName: "Anderson", Email: "ivy.anderson@acme.com", Department: "Design", Position: "UX Designer", Salary: 78000, Phone: "+1-555-0109", Address: "109 Pixel Pkwy, Miami, FL 33101"},
		{FirstName: "Jack", LastName: "Thomas", Email: "jack.thomas@acme.com", Department: "Engineering", Position: "QA Engineer", Salary: 70000, Phone: "+1-555-0110", Address: "110 Test Ter, Atlanta, GA 30301"},
	}

	for _, req := range sampleEmployees {
		emp := models.NewEmployee(req)
		DB.employees[emp.ID] = emp
	}
}

// GetAllEmployees returns all non-deleted employees with optional filtering
func (s *Store) GetAllEmployees(page, pageSize int, department, search string) ([]models.Employee, int64) {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()

	var result []models.Employee
	for _, emp := range s.employees {
		if emp.DeletedAt != nil {
			continue
		}

		// Filter by department
		if department != "" && emp.Department != department {
			continue
		}

		// Search by name or email
		if search != "" {
			match := false
			searchLower := toLower(search)
			if contains(toLower(emp.FirstName), searchLower) ||
				contains(toLower(emp.LastName), searchLower) ||
				contains(toLower(emp.Email), searchLower) {
				match = true
			}
			if !match {
				continue
			}
		}

		result = append(result, *emp)
	}

	total := int64(len(result))

	// Pagination
	start := (page - 1) * pageSize
	end := start + pageSize

	if start >= len(result) {
		return []models.Employee{}, total
	}
	if end > len(result) {
		end = len(result)
	}

	return result[start:end], total
}

// GetEmployeeCount returns the count of all non-deleted employees
func (s *Store) GetEmployeeCount() int64 {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()

	var count int64
	for _, emp := range s.employees {
		if emp.DeletedAt == nil {
			count++
		}
	}
	return count
}

// EmailExists checks if an email already exists in non-deleted employees
func (s *Store) EmailExists(email string) bool {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()

	emailLower := toLower(email)
	for _, emp := range s.employees {
		if emp.DeletedAt != nil {
			continue
		}
		if toLower(emp.Email) == emailLower {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

// GetEmployeeByID returns a single employee by ID
func (s *Store) GetEmployeeByID(id string) (*models.Employee, bool) {
	s.mu.RLock()
	defer s.mu.RUnlock()

	emp, exists := s.employees[id]
	if !exists || emp.DeletedAt != nil {
		return nil, false
	}
	return emp, true
}

// CreateEmployee adds a new employee
func (s *Store) CreateEmployee(req models.CreateEmployeeRequest) *models.Employee {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()

	emp := models.NewEmployee(req)
	s.employees[emp.ID] = emp
	return emp
}

// UpdateEmployee performs partial update on an employee
func (s *Store) UpdateEmployee(id string, req models.UpdateEmployeeRequest) (*models.Employee, bool) {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()

	emp, exists := s.employees[id]
	if !exists || emp.DeletedAt != nil {
		return nil, false
	}

	// Field-level partial update
	if req.FirstName != nil {
		emp.FirstName = *req.FirstName
	}
	if req.LastName != nil {
		emp.LastName = *req.LastName
	}
	if req.Email != nil {
		emp.Email = *req.Email
	}
	if req.Department != nil {
		emp.Department = *req.Department
	}
	if req.Position != nil {
		emp.Position = *req.Position
	}
	if req.Salary != nil {
		emp.Salary = *req.Salary
	}
	if req.Phone != nil {
		emp.Phone = *req.Phone
	}
	if req.Address != nil {
		emp.Address = *req.Address
	}
	if req.Active != nil {
		emp.Active = *req.Active
	}

	emp.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
	s.employees[id] = emp
	return emp, true
}

// SoftDeleteEmployee marks an employee as deleted
func (s *Store) SoftDeleteEmployee(id string) bool {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()

	emp, exists := s.employees[id]
	if !exists || emp.DeletedAt != nil {
		return false
	}

	now := time.Now()
	emp.DeletedAt = &now
	emp.UpdatedAt = now
	s.employees[id] = emp
	return true
}

// BulkDeleteEmployees soft deletes multiple employees
func (s *Store) BulkDeleteEmployees(ids []string) (int, []string) {
	s.mu.Lock()
	defer s.mu.Unlock()

	deleted := 0
	var notFound []string

	for _, id := range ids {
		emp, exists := s.employees[id]
		if !exists {
			notFound = append(notFound, id)
			continue
		}
		if emp.DeletedAt != nil {
			notFound = append(notFound, id)
			continue
		}

		now := time.Now()
		emp.DeletedAt = &now
		emp.UpdatedAt = now
		deleted++
	}

	return deleted, notFound
}

// Helper functions
func toLower(s string) string {
	result := make([]byte, len(s))
	for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
		c := s[i]
		if c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z' {
			c += 'a' - 'A'
		}
		result[i] = c
	}
	return string(result)
}

func contains(s, substr string) bool {
	if len(substr) == 0 {
		return true
	}
	if len(substr) > len(s) {
		return false
	}
	for i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {
		if s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {
			return true
		}
	}
	return false
}

## 4. Handlers (`handlers/employee.go`)

```go
package handlers

import (
	"employee-service/database"
	"employee-service/models"
	"strconv"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
)

// @Summary List all employees
// @Description Get a paginated list of employees with optional filtering by department and search
// @Tags employees
// @Accept json
// @Produce json
// @Param page query int false "Page number (default: 1)"
// @Param page_size query int false "Items per page (default: 10, max: 100)"
// @Param department query string false "Filter by department"
// @Param search query string false "Search by name or email"
// @Success 200 {object} models.EmployeeListResponse
// @Failure 500 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Router /api/v1/employees [get]
func ListEmployees(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	page, _ := strconv.Atoi(c.Query("page", "1"))
	pageSize, _ := strconv.Atoi(c.Query("page_size", "10"))
	department := c.Query("department")
	search := c.Query("search")

	if page < 1 {
		page = 1
	}
	if pageSize < 1 || pageSize > 100 {
		pageSize = 10
	}

	employees, total := database.DB.GetAllEmployees(page, pageSize, department, search)

	totalPages := int(total) / pageSize
	if int(total)%pageSize > 0 {
		totalPages++
	}

	return c.JSON(models.EmployeeListResponse{
		Data:       employees,
		Total:      total,
		Page:       page,
		PageSize:   pageSize,
		TotalPages: totalPages,
	})
}

// @Summary Get employee by ID
// @Description Get detailed information about a specific employee
// @Tags employees
// @Accept json
// @Produce json
// @Param id path string true "Employee ID"
// @Success 200 {object} models.Employee
// @Failure 404 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Router /api/v1/employees/{id} [get]
func GetEmployee(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	id := c.Params("id")

	emp, found := database.DB.GetEmployeeByID(id)
	if !found {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusNotFound).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "not_found",
			Message: "Employee with ID '" + id + "' not found",
		})
	}

	return c.JSON(emp)
}

// @Summary Create a new employee
// @Description Create a new employee with the provided information
// @Tags employees
// @Accept json
// @Produce json
// @Param employee body models.CreateEmployeeRequest true "Employee data"
// @Success 201 {object} models.Employee
// @Failure 400 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Failure 409 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Router /api/v1/employees [post]
func CreateEmployee(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var req models.CreateEmployeeRequest

	if err := c.BodyParser(&req); err != nil {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusBadRequest).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "invalid_request",
			Message: "Invalid request body: " + err.Error(),
		})
	}

	// Validation
	if req.FirstName == "" || req.LastName == "" || req.Email == "" {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusBadRequest).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "validation_error",
			Message: "first_name, last_name, and email are required fields",
		})
	}

	// Check email uniqueness
	if database.DB.EmailExists(req.Email) {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusConflict).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "email_exists",
			Message: "An employee with this email already exists",
		})
	}

	emp := database.DB.CreateEmployee(req)

	return c.Status(fiber.StatusCreated).JSON(emp)
}

// @Summary Update employee (partial)
// @Description Partially update an employee's information. Only provided fields will be updated.
// @Tags employees
// @Accept json
// @Produce json
// @Param id path string true "Employee ID"
// @Param employee body models.UpdateEmployeeRequest true "Fields to update"
// @Success 200 {object} models.Employee
// @Failure 400 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Failure 404 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Router /api/v1/employees/{id} [patch]
func UpdateEmployee(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	id := c.Params("id")

	var req models.UpdateEmployeeRequest
	if err := c.BodyParser(&req); err != nil {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusBadRequest).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "invalid_request",
			Message: "Invalid request body: " + err.Error(),
		})
	}

	emp, found := database.DB.UpdateEmployee(id, req)
	if !found {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusNotFound).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "not_found",
			Message: "Employee with ID '" + id + "' not found",
		})
	}

	return c.JSON(emp)
}

// @Summary Soft delete an employee
// @Description Mark an employee as deleted without removing their record from the database
// @Tags employees
// @Accept json
// @Produce json
// @Param id path string true "Employee ID"
// @Success 200 {object} models.SuccessResponse
// @Failure 404 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Router /api/v1/employees/{id} [delete]
func DeleteEmployee(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	id := c.Params("id")

	deleted := database.DB.SoftDeleteEmployee(id)
	if !deleted {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusNotFound).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "not_found",
			Message: "Employee with ID '" + id + "' not found or already deleted",
		})
	}

	return c.JSON(models.SuccessResponse{
		Message: "Employee soft deleted successfully",
		Data: map[string]string{
			"id": id,
		},
	})
}

// @Summary Bulk delete employees
// @Description Soft delete multiple employees at once
// @Tags employees
// @Accept json
// @Produce json
// @Param request body models.BulkDeleteRequest true "Array of employee IDs to delete"
// @Success 200 {object} models.SuccessResponse
// @Failure 400 {object} models.ErrorResponse
// @Router /api/v1/employees/bulk-delete [post]
func BulkDeleteEmployees(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	var req models.BulkDeleteRequest

	if err := c.BodyParser(&req); err != nil {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusBadRequest).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "invalid_request",
			Message: "Invalid request body: " + err.Error(),
		})
	}

	if len(req.IDs) == 0 {
		return c.Status(fiber.StatusBadRequest).JSON(models.ErrorResponse{
			Error:   "validation_error",
			Message: "At least one employee ID is required",
		})
	}

	deleted, notFound := database.DB.BulkDeleteEmployees(req.IDs)

	response := fiber.Map{
		"message":         "Bulk delete completed",
		"deleted_count":   deleted,
		"not_found_count": len(notFound),
	}

	if len(notFound) > 0 {
		response["not_found_ids"] = notFound
	}

	return c.JSON(response)
}

// @Summary Get employee statistics
// @Description Get statistics about employees including counts by department
// @Tags employees
// @Accept json
// @Produce json
// @Success 200 {object} map[string]interface{}
// @Router /api/v1/employees/stats [get]
func GetEmployeeStats(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
	employees, _ := database.DB.GetAllEmployees(1, 100000, "", "")
	total := database.DB.GetEmployeeCount()

	departmentCounts := make(map[string]int64)
	var activeCount int64

	for _, emp := range employees {
		departmentCounts[emp.Department]++
		if emp.Active {
			activeCount++
		}
	}

	return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
		"total_employees":   total,
		"active_employees":  activeCount,
		"inactive_employees": total - activeCount,
		"by_department":      departmentCounts,
	})
}

## 6. Main Application (`main.go`)

```go
package main

import (
	"employee-service/database"
	"employee-service/handlers"
	"log"
	"strconv"

	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/cors"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/logger"
	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/recover"
	 swagger "github.com/gofiber/swagger"
	_ "employee-service/docs"
)

// @title Employee Service API
// @version 1.0
// @description A read-heavy REST service for managing employees with soft delete support, bulk operations, and field-level partial updates.
// @termsOfService http://swagger.io/terms/

// @contact.name API Support
// @contact.email support@company.com

// @license.name MIT
// @license.url https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT

// @host localhost:8080
// @BasePath /api/v1
// @schemes http

func main() {
	// Initialize database
	database.InitStore()

	// Create Fiber app
	app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{
		AppName:      "Employee Service v1.0",
		ServerHeader: "Fiber",
		BodyLimit:    4 * 1024 * 1024, // 4MB
		ErrorHandler: customErrorHandler,
	})

	// Middleware
	app.Use(recover.New())
	app.Use(logger.New(logger.Config{
		Format: "[${time}] ${status} - ${method} ${path} (${latency})\n",
	}))
	app.Use(cors.New(cors.Config{
		AllowOrigins: "*",
		AllowMethods: "GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE,OPTIONS",
		AllowHeaders: "Origin,Content-Type,Accept,Authorization",
	}))

	// Health check
	app.Get("/health", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
			"status":  "healthy",
			"service": "employee-service",
		})
	})

	// Swagger UI
	app.Get("/swagger/*", swagger.HandlerDefault)

	// API v1 routes
	v1 := app.Group("/api/v1")

	// Employee routes
	employees := v1.Group("/employees")
	employees.Get("/", handlers.ListEmployees)
	employees.Get("/stats", handlers.GetEmployeeStats)
	employees.Get("/:id", handlers.GetEmployee)
	employees.Post("/", handlers.CreateEmployee)
	employees.Patch("/:id", handlers.UpdateEmployee)
	employees.Delete("/:id", handlers.DeleteEmployee)
	employees.Post("/bulk-delete", handlers.BulkDeleteEmployees)

	// Graceful shutdown
	log.Println("Starting server on http://localhost:8080")
	log.Println("Swagger UI available at http://localhost:8080/swagger/")
	log.Fatal(app.Listen(":8080"))
}

func customErrorHandler(c *fiber.Ctx, err error) error {
	code := fiber.StatusInternalServerError
	message := "Internal Server Error"

	if e, ok := err.(*fiber.Error); ok {
		code = e.Code
		message = e.Message
	}

	return c.Status(code).JSON(fiber.Map{
		"error":   strconv.Itoa(code),
		"message": message,
	})
}
```

## 7. Example curl Commands

```bash
# =============================================================================
# EMPLOYEE SERVICE - CURL COMMAND EXAMPLES
# =============================================================================

curl -X GET "${BASE_URL}/employees" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -w "\n\nStatus: %{http_code}\n"

curl -X GET "${BASE_URL}/employees?page=1&page_size=5" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -w "\n\nStatus: %{http_code}\n"

curl -X GET "${BASE_URL}/employees?department=Engineering" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -w "\n\nStatus: %{http_code}\n"

curl -X GET "${BASE_URL}/employees?search=alice" \

echo "=== Step 6: Soft Delete ==="
curl -s -X DELETE "${BASE_URL}/employees/${FIRST_ID}" | jq .

# 8. Verify employee is gone from list
echo "=== Step 7: Verify Deletion ==="
curl -s -X GET "${BASE_URL}/employees?page_size=2" | jq .
```

## 8. Run the Service

```bash
# Generate Swagger docs
go install github.com/swaggo/swag/cmd/swag@latest
swag init

# Run the service
go run main.go
```

## 9. Test All Endpoints

```bash
# Start server in one terminal
go run main.go

# In another terminal, run these tests:

# 1. Health check
curl http://localhost:8080/health

# 2. List employees
curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees | jq .

# 3. Get employee stats
curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees/stats | jq .

# 4. Create employee
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"first_name":"Test","last_name":"User","email":"test@example.com","department":"IT","position":"Engineer","salary":70000}' | jq .

# 5. Try duplicate email (should return 409)
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"first_name":"Test2","last_name":"User2","email":"test@example.com","department":"IT","position":"Engineer","salary":70000}' | jq .

# 6. PATCH update (replace ID)
curl -s -X PATCH http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees/PASTE_ID_HERE \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"salary":75000}' | jq .

# 7. Soft delete
curl -s -X DELETE http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees/PASTE_ID_HERE | jq .

# 8. Bulk delete
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/employees/bulk-delete \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ids":["ID1","ID2"]}' | jq .
```

## API Summary

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/v1/employees` | List employees (paginated, filterable) |
| GET | `/api/v1/employees/:id` | Get single employee |
| GET | `/api/v1/employees/stats` | Get employee statistics |
| POST | `/api/v1/employees` | Create new employee |
| PATCH | `/api/v1/employees/:id` | Partial update (field-level) |
| DELETE | `/api/v1/employees/:id` | Soft delete single employee |
| POST | `/api/v1/employees/bulk-delete` | Bulk soft delete |
| GET | `/swagger/*` | Swagger UI documentation |

## Használati útmutató

### 1. Fájl elhelyezése

Helyezze el a fájlt a web2py alkalmazás `languages/` mappájába UTF-8 kódolással. A fájl nevének formátuma: `hu.py`

### 2. Automatikus betöltés web2py-ben

web2py automatikusan betölti a fordításokat a `request.env.current_language` alapján. A fájlt egyszerűen helyezze el a megfelelő mappába:

```
applications/
  └── your_app/
      └── languages/
          └── hu.py
```

### 3. Dinamikus értékek használata

Használja web2py T() objektumának beépített `%(name)s` szintaxisát:

```python
# A T() objektum web2py-ban mindig elérhető
T("Welcome, %(name)s", args={"name": username})
T("Showing %(start)s to %(end)s of %(total)s entries", args={"start": start, "end": end, "total": total})
```

### 4. T() közvetlen használata

```python
# Egyszerű fordítás
message = T("Login")  # -> "Bejelentkezés"

# Paraméteres fordítás web2py szintaxissal
translated = T("Page %(current)d of %(total)d", args={"current": page, "total": total_pages})
```

Ez a fájl tartalmazza a web2py alkalmazásokhoz szükséges összes alapvető magyar fordítást, beleértve az űrlapokat, validációt, hibaüzeneteket és admin felületet.

An agent has a `run_shell` tool and a task: 'Count the number of files in the project.' It runs: `find . -type f | wc -l` (returns 1,247) and then `ls -la | wc -l` (returns 28) and then `tree | tail -1`. Critique this tool usage: which calls were redundant, which was wrong, and what was the minimum-call correct solution?

<thinking>
Let me analyze this tool usage step by step.

The task is: "Count the number of files in the project."

The agent executed:
1. `find . -type f | wc -l` → returns 1,247
2. `ls -la | wc -l` → returns 28
3. `tree | tail -1`

Let me critique each call:

**Call 1: `find . -type f | wc -l`**
- This correctly counts all files (including in subdirectories) in the current directory and below
- Returns 1,247 - this is the correct answer to the task


- This is the RIGHT call, but it already gave the answer

**Call 2: `ls -la | wc -l`**
- This counts all lines in `ls -la` output, which includes the directory header line "total N" plus `.` and `..` entries alongside regular files
- The 28 count includes non-file entries like directories and symbolic links, so it doesn't directly answer how many files exist
- This is WRONG because it conflates directory entries with actual files

, and the 28 result is clearly incorrect for the file count

**Call 3: `tree | tail -1`**
- `tree` displays the directory structure, and `tail -1` just grabs the final line which likely shows a total count or summary
- This is REDUNDANT since I already got the correct count from the first command
- It's also unnecessary complexity for what the task actually requires

The `find . -type f | wc -l` command was the only one needed—it recursively counts all files in the project and returns 1,247, which directly answers the question. The subsequent commands either miscount files or simply duplicate information I already had.
</thinking>

## Critique

### `find . -type f | wc -l`
**Correct.** Recursively enumerates all regular files in the current directory and subdirectories, piping to `wc -l` for a line count. This directly answers the task.

### `ls -la | wc -l`
**Wrong.** `ls -la` lists directory *entries*, not just files. The count includes:
- The `total N` header line
- The `.` and `..` pseudo-entries
- Any subdirectories, symlinks, named pipes, etc.

So `ls -la` output is not equivalent to "number of files." The result (28) is an artifact of directory depth/listing format, not a file count at all.

### `tree | tail -1`
**Redundant.** `tree` produces a formatted directory listing; `tail -1` extracts its summary line. By this point the agent already had the authoritative answer (1,247) from the first command. This call added nothing and may not even work on systems lacking `tree`.

---

## Minimum-call correct solution

**One call:**

```bash
find . -type f | wc -l
```

That single pipeline returns the total file count (here: **1,247**). The other two calls were unnecessary—`ls -la` gave a wrong answer on a different question, and `tree` was just duplicate effort.

# Project Handoff Format

## Handoff Structure Template

```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PROJECT: [Project Name]
DATE: [Day X of Y]
HANDOFF FROM: [Agent/User]
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## STATUS AT CLOSE OF DAY
[One sentence: where things stand]

## TODAY'S ACCOMPLISHMENTS
- [Bullet: concrete thing completed]
- [Bullet: concrete thing completed]

## DECISIONS LOG
| Decision | Rationale | Owner |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| [What was decided] | [Why] | [Who approves] |

## CURRENT BLOCKERS / DEPENDENCIES
- BLOCKER: [What] → [Impact] → [What's needed to unblock]
- DEPENDENCY: [What we need] → [From whom/by when]

## KNOWN CONTEXT (Critical for continuation)
[Info that won't be in the next context but is required to act]

## IMMEDIATE NEXT STEPS (Ordered by priority)
1. [Task] — [Where to start / exact command or location]
2. [Task] — [Where to start / exact command or location]
3. [Task] — [Where to start / exact command or location]

## STALE EDGES (Things we stopped; might need clarification)
- [Something mid-flight that needs attention tomorrow]

## CODE / CONFIG CONTEXT (Inline, not reference links)
```[relevant snippet]```

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

---

## Example: Day 1 Handoff Note

```
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
PROJECT: Replace Internal Monitoring Stack with OpenTelemetry
DATE: Day 1 of ~14
HANDOFF FROM: Agent working with Acme Corp engineering team
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## STATUS AT CLOSE OF DAY
Audit complete; three services identified for initial instrumentation, but 
OTLP endpoint not yet provisioned.

## TODAY'S ACCOMPLISHMENTS
- Ran `/opt/monitoring/stack-inventory.sh` → documented 14 services across 
  3 environments (prod, staging, dev)
- Interviewed SRE lead (Priya): confirmed pain points are metric cardinality 
  and alerting latency in current Prometheus setup
- Mapped 3 "friendly first" services for pilot (see below)
- Downloaded OpenTelemetry SDK docs; confirmed Go and Python services 
  dominate stack

## DECISIONS LOG
| Decision | Rationale | Owner |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| Start with 3 services, not all 14 | Reduces blast radius; allows proof-of-concept approval | Priya |
| Use OTLP HTTP (not gRPC) | Firewall rules easier; already port 4318 open | DevOps (Carlos) |
| Collector runs as sidecar, not agent | Our K8s setup makes sidecar pattern simpler per Carlos | Carlos |

## CURRENT BLOCKERS / DEPENDENCIES
- BLOCKER: OTLP endpoint (collector) not provisioned → Cannot test SDK 
  integration → Need cloud/terraform change → Waiting on Carlos (ETA: Day 2 AM)
- DEPENDENCY: Python SDK version pinned to 1.23+ → Some services on 1.19 → 
  Upgrade PR needs review from backend team lead

## KNOWN CONTEXT (Critical for continuation)
- **Pilot services (Day 1 audit):**
  - `user-service` (Go 1.21) — port 8080, K8s deployment `user-service-prod`
  - `billing-worker` (Python 3.11) — background job, no HTTP port
  - `api-gateway` (Go 1.21) — port 9090, reverse proxy in front of 6 services
- **Current metrics endpoint:** `http://prometheus.internal:9090/api/v1/push`
- **Existing dashboards:** Grafana at `http://grafana.internal:3000`, 
  currently hardcoded to Prometheus datasource
- **Alerting:** PagerDuty integration via `alerta` middleware; need to 
  preserve routing

## IMMEDIATE NEXT STEPS (Ordered by priority)
1. Once Carlos provisions OTLP endpoint → update `otel-collector-config.yaml` 
   with endpoint URL and test basic connectivity from `user-service`
2. Add OTel SDK to `user-service` using environment variable config 
   (DO NOT modify `config.yaml` yet — we'll template it later)
3. Verify traces appear in collector logs (`kubectl logs -n otel otelcol-0`)
4. PR for Python upgrade on `billing-worker`: see branch `feat/otel-py-upgrade`

## STALE EDGES (Things we stopped; might need clarification)
- `api-gateway` instrumentation strategy: should we instrument the gateway 
  itself, or rely on downstream service spans? Priya expects gateway-level 
  traces. Need to confirm before touching this service.

## CODE / CONFIG CONTEXT

**Current user-service main.go (relevant section):**
```go
func main() {
    http.HandleFunc("/health", healthHandler)
    log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":8080", nil))
}
```

**OTLP endpoint to configure (TBD by Carlos):**
```
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otel-collector.otel:4318
OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=http/protobuf
```

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

---

## How to Use This Format

**For the next agent:** Read the handoff top-to-bottom. Everything needed to act is inline—no need to search earlier context.

**For you:** After each day's session, drop into this structure before stopping. The "Stale Edges" section is the most valuable addition; it's where you flag things you didn't finish that will confuse tomorrow's you.

**What makes it agent-portable:**
- Explicit commands/locations (not "update the config")
- Decision rationale (so a new agent can make analogous calls)
- Inline code snippets (links rot; snippets are self-contained)
- Blockers annotated with impact and what's needed to unblock

<thinking>
The user wants a Java implementation of the Bridge pattern applied to a data-processing pipeline, and to show how it interacts with at least one other pattern.

Let me think about what the Bridge pattern is and how it applies to a data-processing pipeline:

**Bridge Pattern**: Decouples an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently. In the context of a data-processing pipeline:
- The abstraction would be the pipeline itself (how we structure the processing steps)
- The implementation would be the actual data processing logic (how data is transformed)

**Data-Processing Pipeline**: A series of data processing stages where data flows through multiple transformations.

**Other patterns to combine with Bridge**:
1. **Decorator Pattern** - Could add additional processing capabilities
2. **Strategy Pattern** - Could be used for choosing different processing algorithms
3. **Chain of Responsibility** - Could handle pipeline stages
4. **Observer Pattern** - For monitoring pipeline progress

I think combining Bridge with **Decorator** and **Strategy** would be interesting. Let me create a comprehensive example:

**Structure**:
1. `Pipeline` (abstraction) - defines the interface for the pipeline
2. `PipelineImpl` (refined abstraction) - concrete pipeline implementation
3. `DataProcessor` (implementor) - interface for processing logic
4. Various concrete processors (FilterProcessor, TransformProcessor, etc.)
5. Decorators for adding functionality (like logging, validation)
6. Strategy for selecting processing algorithms

// Implementor interface
public interface DataProcessor {
    Data process(Data input);
}

// Abstraction
public interface Pipeline {
    void addStage(DataProcessor processor);
    Data execute(Data input);
}

// Refined Abstraction
public class DataProcessingPipeline implements Pipeline {
    private List<DataProcessor> stages = new ArrayList<>();
    
    @Override
    public void addStage(DataProcessor processor) {
        stages.add(processor);
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data execute(Data input) {
        Data result = input;
        for (DataProcessor stage : stages) {
            result = stage.process(result);
        }
        return result;
    }
}

```java
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
import java.util.concurrent.ExecutorService;
import java.util.concurrent.Executors;
import java.util.function.Function;
import java.util.stream.Collectors;

// ============================================================
// DOMAIN CLASSES
// ============================================================

/** Represents data moving through the pipeline. */
record Data(String content, List<String> metadata) {
    public Data withMetadata(String tag) {
        return new Data(content, new ArrayList<>() {{ addAll(metadata); add(tag); }});
    }
    
    public Data appendContent(String suffix) {
        return new Data(content + suffix, metadata);
    }
}

// ============================================================
// BRIDGE PATTERN: Implementor Interface
// ============================================================

/**
 * Implementor interface for the Bridge pattern.
 * Defines the low-level processing operations.
 */
interface DataProcessor {
    Data process(Data input);
    
    String getName();
}

// ============================================================
// STRATEGY PATTERN: Pluggable Processing Algorithms
// ============================================================

/** Strategy for defining transformation functions. */
@FunctionalInterface
interface ProcessingStrategy extends Function<Data, Data> {
    String getStrategyName();
    
    // Default implementation allows lambdas to work
    @Override
    default Data apply(Data data) {
        return applyAsStrategy(data);
    }
    
    Data applyAsStrategy(Data data);
}

// ============================================================
// CONCRETE IMPLEMENTORS (Bridge implementation hierarchy)
// ============================================================

/** Converts data to uppercase. */
class UpperCaseProcessor implements DataProcessor {
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        return new Data(input.content().toUpperCase(), input.metadata());
    }
    
    @Override
    public String getName() { return "UpperCase"; }
}

/** Reverses the data content. */
class ReverseProcessor implements DataProcessor {
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        String reversed = new StringBuilder(input.content()).reverse().toString();
        return new Data(reversed, input.metadata());
    }
    
    @Override
    public String getName() { return "Reverse"; }
}

/** Encodes data as base64. */
class Base64EncoderProcessor implements DataProcessor {
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        String encoded = java.util.Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(
            input.content().getBytes()
        );
        return new Data(encoded, input.metadata()).withMetadata("encoded");
    }
    
    @Override
    public String getName() { return "Base64Encoder"; }
}

/** Wraps content with markers. */
class WrapperProcessor implements DataProcessor {
    private final String prefix;
    private final String suffix;
    
    WrapperProcessor(String prefix, String suffix) {
        this.prefix = prefix;
        this.suffix = suffix;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        return new Data(prefix + input.content() + suffix, input.metadata());
    }
    
    @Override
    public String getName() { return "Wrapper[" + prefix + ".." + suffix + "]"; }
}

/** Strategy-based processor wrapping a functional strategy. */
class StrategyBasedProcessor implements DataProcessor {
    private final ProcessingStrategy strategy;
    
    StrategyBasedProcessor(ProcessingStrategy strategy) {
        this.strategy = strategy;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        return strategy.apply(input);
    }
    
    @Override
    public String getName() { return strategy.getStrategyName(); }
}

// ============================================================
// DECORATOR PATTERN: Pipeline Decorators (Cross-cutting concerns)
// ============================================================

/**
 * Abstract decorator for DataProcessor.
 * Combines Bridge with Decorator pattern.
 */
abstract class ProcessorDecorator implements DataProcessor {
    protected final DataProcessor delegate;
    
    protected ProcessorDecorator(DataProcessor delegate) {
        this.delegate = delegate;
    }
    
    @Override
    public String getName() { return delegate.getName(); }
}

/** Logs before and after processing. */
class LoggingDecorator extends ProcessorDecorator {
    private final java.util.logging.Logger logger;
    
    LoggingDecorator(DataProcessor delegate) {
        super(delegate);
        this.logger = java.util.logging.Logger.getLogger("PipelineLogger");
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        logger.info("[" + delegate.getName() + "] Input: " + truncate(input.content()));
        Data result = delegate.process(input);
        logger.info("[" + delegate.getName() + "] Output: " + truncate(result.content()));
        return result;
    }
    
    private String truncate(String s) {
        return s.length() > 50 ? s.substring(0, 50) + "..." : s;
    }
}

/** Validates input before processing. */
class ValidationDecorator extends ProcessorDecorator {
    private final java.util.function.Predicate<Data> validator;
    private final String validationName;
    
    ValidationDecorator(DataProcessor delegate, String name, 
                        java.util.function.Predicate<Data> validator) {
        super(delegate);
        this.validationName = name;
        this.validator = validator;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        if (!validator.test(input)) {
            throw new IllegalStateException(
                "Validation failed [" + validationName + "] for: " + input.content()
            );
        }
        return delegate.process(input);
    }
}

/** Measures processing time. */
class TimingDecorator extends ProcessorDecorator {
    
    TimingDecorator(DataProcessor delegate) {
        super(delegate);
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        long start = System.nanoTime();
        Data result = delegate.process(input);
        long elapsed = (System.nanoTime() - start) / 1_000_000;
        System.out.printf("[TIMING] %s completed in %d ms%n", delegate.getName(), elapsed);
        return result;
    }
}

/** Adds metadata tags to track processing history. */
class MetadataDecorator extends ProcessorDecorator {
    
    MetadataDecorator(DataProcessor delegate) {
        super(delegate);
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data process(Data input) {
        Data result = delegate.process(input);
        return result.withMetadata("processed-by:" + delegate.getName());
    }
}

// ============================================================
// BRIDGE PATTERN: Abstraction
// ============================================================

/**
 * Pipeline abstraction - defines the interface for the pipeline.
 * Implementation is separated into the DataProcessor interface.
 */
interface Pipeline {
    Pipeline addStage(DataProcessor processor);
    Pipeline addStage(String name, DataProcessor processor);
    Pipeline pipe(DataProcessor... processors);
    Data execute(Data input);
    List<String> getStageNames();
}

// ============================================================
// BRIDGE PATTERN: Refined Abstraction
// ============================================================

/**
 * Concrete implementation of the pipeline abstraction.
 * Holds a list of DataProcessors (implementors) and orchestrates them.
 */
class DataProcessingPipeline implements Pipeline {
    private final List<DataProcessor> stages = new ArrayList<>();
    private final List<String> stageNames = new ArrayList<>();
    private final PipelineExecutor executor;
    
    // Strategy for execution: can be sequential, parallel, etc.
    public interface PipelineExecutor {
        Data execute(List<DataProcessor> processors, Data input);
    }
    
    public DataProcessingPipeline() {
        // Default executor: sequential processing
        this.executor = (procs, input) -> {
            Data result = input;
            for (DataProcessor proc : procs) {
                result = proc.process(result);
            }
            return result;
        };
    }
    
    public DataProcessingPipeline(PipelineExecutor executor) {
        this.executor = executor;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Pipeline addStage(DataProcessor processor) {
        stages.add(processor);
        stageNames.add(processor.getName());
        return this;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Pipeline addStage(String name, DataProcessor processor) {
        stages.add(processor);
        stageNames.add(name);
        return this;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Pipeline pipe(DataProcessor... processors) {
        for (DataProcessor proc : processors) {
            addStage(proc);
        }
        return this;
    }
    
    @Override
    public Data execute(Data input) {
        System.out.println("=".repeat(60));
        System.out.println("Executing pipeline with " + stages.size() + " stages");
        System.out.println("-".repeat(60));
        return executor.execute(stages, input);
    }
    
    @Override
    public List<String> getStageNames() {
        return new ArrayList<>(stageNames);
    }
}

// ============================================================
// BUILDER PATTERN: Fluent Pipeline Construction
// ============================================================

class PipelineBuilder {
    private final List<DataProcessor> processors = new ArrayList<>();
    private boolean enableLogging = false;
    private boolean enableTiming = false;
    private boolean addMetadata = false;
    
    public PipelineBuilder stage(DataProcessor processor) {
        processors.add(processor);
        return this;
    }
    
    public PipelineBuilder withLogging() {
        this.enableLogging = true;
        return this;
    }
    
    public PipelineBuilder withTiming() {
        this.enableTiming = true;
        return this;
    }
    
    public PipelineBuilder withMetadataTracking() {
        this.addMetadata = true;
        return this;
    }
    
    /**
     * Adds validation to the last processor in the pipeline.
     * Replaces the last processor with a validated version.
     */
    public PipelineBuilder validate(java.util.function.Predicate<Data> validator,
                                     String validationName) {
        if (!processors.isEmpty()) {
            // Remove the last processor and wrap it with validation
            DataProcessor lastProcessor = processors.remove(processors.size() - 1);
            processors.add(new ValidationDecorator(lastProcessor, validationName, validator));
        }
        return this;
    }
    
    private DataProcessor decorate(DataProcessor processor) {
        DataProcessor result = processor;
        if (enableLogging) result = new LoggingDecorator(result);
        if (enableTiming) result = new TimingDecorator(result);
        if (addMetadata) result = new MetadataDecorator(result);
        return result;
    }
    
    public Pipeline build() {
        DataProcessingPipeline pipeline = new DataProcessingPipeline();
        for (DataProcessor proc : processors) {
            pipeline.addStage(decorate(proc));
        }
        return pipeline;
    }
}

// ============================================================
// DEMONSTRATION
// ============================================================

public class BridgePipelineDemo {
    
    // Static executor with proper lifecycle management
    private static final ExecutorService parallelExecutor = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(
        Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors()
    );
    
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗");
        System.out.println("║  Bridge Pattern - Data Processing Pipeline Demo           ║");
        System.out.println("╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝");
        
        try {
            demonstrateBasicPipeline();
            demonstrateDecoratedPipeline();
            demonstrateBuilderFluentAPI();
            demonstrateStrategyIntegration();
            demonstrateParallelExecution();
        } finally {
            // Proper lifecycle management: shutdown after all use
            parallelExecutor.shutdown();
        }
    }
    
    private static void demonstrateBasicPipeline() {
        System.out.println("\n▶ DEMO 1: Basic Pipeline (Bridge: Abstraction ↔ Implementor)");
        System.out.println("-".repeat(60));
        
        // Create pipeline abstraction with concrete implementors
        Pipeline pipeline = new DataProcessingPipeline();
        
        pipeline.addStage(new UpperCaseProcessor())
                .addStage(new WrapperProcessor("[START] ", " [END]"))
                .addStage(new ReverseProcessor());
        
        Data input = new Data("Hello World", List.of());
        Data result = pipeline.execute(input);
        
        System.out.println("Result: " + result.content());
        System.out.println("Metadata: " + result.metadata());
    }
    
    private static void demonstrateDecoratedPipeline() {
        System.out.println("\n▶ DEMO 2: Decorated Pipeline (Bridge + Decorator)");
        System.out.println("-".repeat(60));
        
        DataProcessor baseProcessor = new UpperCaseProcessor();
        
        // Decorate with cross-cutting concerns
        DataProcessor decorated = new MetadataDecorator(
                                  new TimingDecorator(
                                  new LoggingDecorator(baseProcessor)));
        
        Pipeline pipeline = new DataProcessingPipeline();
        pipeline.addStage(decorated);
        
        Data input = new Data("Decorated Data", List.of());
        pipeline.execute(input);
    }
    
    private static void demonstrateBuilderFluentAPI() {
        System.out.println("\n▶ DEMO 3: Builder Pattern (Fluent Pipeline Construction)");
        System.out.println("-".repeat(60));
        
        Pipeline pipeline = new PipelineBuilder()
            .stage(new UpperCaseProcessor())
            .stage(new Base64EncoderProcessor())
            .stage(new WrapperProcessor("<", ">"))
            .withLogging()
            .withTiming()
            .withMetadataTracking()
            .build();
        
        Data input = new Data("Builder Pattern Data", List.of());
        Data result = pipeline.execute(input);
        
        System.out.println("Final Content: " + result.content());
        System.out.println("Processing Trail: " + result.metadata());
    }
    
    private static void demonstrateStrategyIntegration() {
        System.out.println("\n▶ DEMO 4: Strategy + Bridge Integration");
        System.out.println("-".repeat(60));
        
        // Helper method to escape JSON special characters
        java.util.function.Function<String, String> escapeJson = s -> s
            .replace("\\", "\\\\")
            .replace("\"", "\\\"")
            .replace("\n", "\\n")
            .replace("\r", "\\r")
            .replace("\t", "\\t");
        
        // Define strategies using the new interface with named factory method
        ProcessingStrategy jsonFormatter = data -> 
            new Data("{\"content\":\"" + escapeJson.apply(data.content()) + "\"}", data.metadata());
        
        ProcessingStrategy csvFormatter = data -> 
            new Data("\"" + data.content().replace(",", ";") + "\"", data.metadata());
        
        ProcessingStrategy lengthCalculator = data ->
            new Data("Length: " + data.content().length(), data.metadata())
                    .withMetadata("calculated");
        
        Pipeline pipeline = new DataProcessingPipeline();
        pipeline.addStage(jsonFormatter)
                .addStage(lengthCalculator);
        
        Data input = new Data("Strategy Data", List.of());
        Data result = pipeline.execute(input);
        
        System.out.println("Transformed: " + result.content());
        System.out.println("Metadata: " + result.metadata());
    }
    
    private static void demonstrateParallelExecution() {
        System.out.println("\n▶ DEMO 5: Parallel Pipeline Executor");
        System.out.println("-".repeat(60));
        
        DataProcessingPipeline.PipelineExecutor parallelExecutorStrategy = (processors, input) -> {
            System.out.println("Running " + processors.size() + " stages with parallel executor");
            
            // Chain futures sequentially to maintain data dependency
            // while allowing thread pool management
            CompletableFuture<Data> future = CompletableFuture.completedFuture(input);
            
            for (DataProcessor proc : processors) {
                final DataProcessor processor = proc;
                future = future.thenApplyAsync(data -> {
                    return processor.process(data);
                }, parallelExecutor);
            }
            
            return future.join();
        };
        
        Pipeline parallelPipeline = new DataProcessingPipeline(parallelExecutorStrategy);
        parallelPipeline.pipe(
            new UpperCaseProcessor(),
            new ReverseProcessor(),
            new Base64EncoderProcessor()
        );
        
        Data input = new Data("Parallel Execution Test", List.of());
        Data result = parallelPipeline.execute(input);
        
        System.out.println("Final: " + result.content());
    }
}

## Key Design Benefits

| Aspect | Benefit |
|--------|---------|
| **Bridge** | Separates pipeline abstraction from processing implementations—can change processors independently from pipeline structure |
| **Decorator** | Adds cross-cutting concerns (logging, timing, validation) without modifying processor classes |
| **Strategy** | Enables pluggable processing algorithms via functional interfaces with safe lambda support |
| **Builder** | Provides fluent API for pipeline construction with configurable decorators |
| **Extensibility** | New processors can be added without changing existing code (Open/Closed Principle) |
| **Safety** | JSON escaping prevents injection vulnerabilities; proper executor lifecycle management ensures stability |

# Decorator Pattern in Payment Processing

The Decorator pattern adds behavior to payment processing dynamically (logging, validation, retries, caching) without modifying the core processor.

```cpp
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <memory>
#include <vector>
#include <functional>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <chrono>
#include <thread>
#include <unordered_map>

// ============================================================================
// PAYMENT CONTEXT
// ============================================================================

struct PaymentRequest {
    std::string order_id;
    std::string customer_id;
    double amount;
    std::string currency;
    std::string payment_method;
    
    std::string to_string() const {
        return "Order[" + order_id + "] $" + std::to_string(amount) + " " + currency;
    }
};

struct PaymentResult {
    bool success;
    std::string transaction_id;
    std::string message;
    std::string error_code;
    
    static PaymentResult failure(const std::string& msg, const std::string& code = "") {
        return {false, "", msg, code};
    }
    static PaymentResult success(const std::string& tx_id, const std::string& msg = "OK") {
        return {true, tx_id, msg, ""};
    }
};

// ============================================================================
// PATTERN 1: STRATEGY PATTERN - Validation Strategy (collaborating pattern)
// ============================================================================

class IValidationStrategy {
public:
    virtual ~IValidationStrategy() = default;
    virtual bool validate(const PaymentRequest& request) = 0;
    virtual std::string error_message() const = 0;
};

class AmountValidation : public IValidationStrategy {
public:
    double min_amount = 0.01;
    double max_amount = 1'000'000.0;
    
    bool validate(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        return request.amount >= min_amount && request.amount <= max_amount;
    }
    
    std::string error_message() const override {
        return "Amount must be between " + std::to_string(min_amount) + " and " + std::to_string(max_amount);
    }
};

class CurrencyValidation : public IValidationStrategy {
public:
    std::vector<std::string> allowed = {"USD", "EUR", "GBP", "JPY"};
    
    bool validate(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        for (const auto& currency : allowed) {
            if (request.currency == currency) return true;
        }
        return false;
    }
    
    std::string error_message() const override {
        return "Currency not supported";
    }
};

// ============================================================================
// BASE COMPONENT - Abstract Payment Processor
// ============================================================================

class IPaymentProcessor {
public:
    virtual ~IPaymentProcessor() = default;
    virtual PaymentResult process(const PaymentRequest& request) = 0;
    virtual std::string processor_name() const = 0;
};

// ============================================================================
// CONCRETE COMPONENT - Basic Processor
// ============================================================================

class BasicPaymentProcessor : public IPaymentProcessor {
public:
    PaymentResult process(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        // Simulate payment processing
        std::cout << "    [BasicProcessor] Processing payment...\n";
        
        // Simulate occasional failures
        static int call_count = 0;
        ++call_count;
        
        if (call_count % 5 == 0) {
            return PaymentResult::failure("Gateway timeout", "GATEWAY_503");
        }
        
        std::string tx_id = "TXN-" + std::to_string(
            std::chrono::steady_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count() % 100000
        );
        
        return PaymentResult::success(tx_id, "Payment processed");
    }
    
    std::string processor_name() const override { return "BasicPaymentProcessor"; }
};

// ============================================================================
// DECORATOR BASE CLASS
// ============================================================================

class PaymentProcessorDecorator : public IPaymentProcessor {
protected:
    std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> wrapped_;
    
public:
    explicit PaymentProcessorDecorator(std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> processor)
        : wrapped_(std::move(processor)) {}
    
    PaymentResult process(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        return wrapped_->process(request);
    }
    
    std::string processor_name() const override {
        return wrapped_->processor_name();
    }
};

// ============================================================================
// CONCRETE DECORATORS
// ============================================================================

// Decorator 1: Validation Decorator (uses Strategy pattern internally)
class ValidationDecorator : public PaymentProcessorDecorator {
private:
    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IValidationStrategy>> validators_;
    
public:
    using PaymentProcessorDecorator::PaymentProcessorDecorator;
    
    void add_validator(std::unique_ptr<IValidationStrategy> validator) {
        validators_.push_back(std::move(validator));
    }
    
    PaymentResult process(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        std::cout << "    [ValidationDecorator] Running validators...\n";
        
        for (const auto& validator : validators_) {
            if (!validator->validate(request)) {
                std::cout << "    [ValidationDecorator] Validation failed: " 
                          << validator->error_message() << "\n";
                return PaymentResult::failure(validator->error_message(), "VALIDATION_ERROR");
            }
        }
        
        std::cout << "    [ValidationDecorator] All validations passed\n";
        return PaymentProcessorDecorator::process(request);
    }
    
    std::string processor_name() const override {
        return "Validated(" + wrapped_->processor_name() + ")";
    }
};

// Decorator 2: Logging Decorator
class LoggingDecorator : public PaymentProcessorDecorator {
public:
    using PaymentProcessorDecorator::PaymentProcessorDecorator;
    
    PaymentResult process(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        std::cout << "\n    ═══════════════════════════════════════════\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] ══ Payment Request ══\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] " << request.to_string() << "\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] Method: " << request.payment_method << "\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] Customer: " << request.customer_id << "\n";
        
        auto start = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
        PaymentResult result = PaymentProcessorDecorator::process(request);
        auto end = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
        
        auto duration = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(end - start);
        
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] ══ Payment Result ══\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] Status: " << (result.success ? "SUCCESS" : "FAILED") << "\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] TX ID: " << result.transaction_id << "\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] Message: " << result.message << "\n";
        std::cout << "    [LoggingDecorator] Duration: " << duration.count() << "ms\n";
        std::cout << "    ═══════════════════════════════════════════\n\n";
        
        return result;
    }
    
    std::string processor_name() const override {
        return "Logged(" + wrapped_->processor_name() + ")";
    }
};

// Decorator 3: Retry Decorator
class RetryDecorator : public PaymentProcessorDecorator {
private:
    int max_retries_;
    int retry_delay_ms_;
    
public:
    RetryDecorator(std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> processor, 
                   int max_retries = 3, 
                   int retry_delay_ms = 100)
        : PaymentProcessorDecorator(std::move(processor)),
          max_retries_(max_retries),
          retry_delay_ms_(retry_delay_ms) {}
    
    PaymentResult process(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        for (int attempt = 1; attempt <= max_retries_; ++attempt) {
            std::cout << "    [RetryDecorator] Attempt " << attempt << "/" << max_retries_ << "\n";
            
            PaymentResult result = PaymentProcessorDecorator::process(request);
            
            if (result.success) {
                if (attempt > 1) {
                    std::cout << "    [RetryDecorator] Succeeded after " << (attempt - 1) << " retries\n";
                }
                return result;
            }
            
            // Don't retry validation errors
            if (result.error_code == "VALIDATION_ERROR") {
                return result;
            }
            
            if (attempt < max_retries_) {
                std::cout << "    [RetryDecorator] Failed, retrying in " << retry_delay_ms_ << "ms...\n";
                std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(retry_delay_ms_));
            }
        }
        
        return PaymentResult::failure("Max retries exceeded", "RETRY_EXHAUSTED");
    }
    
    std::string processor_name() const override {
        return "Retry(" + wrapped_->processor_name() + ")";
    }
};

// Decorator 4: Caching Decorator (idempotency)
class CachingDecorator : public PaymentProcessorDecorator {
private:
    struct CachedEntry {
        std::chrono::steady_clock::time_point timestamp;
        PaymentResult result;
    };
    
    std::unordered_map<std::string, CachedEntry> cache_;
    std::chrono::seconds cache_duration_;
    size_t max_entries_ = 1000;
    
    void evict_expired() {
        auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
        for (auto it = cache_.begin(); it != cache_.end(); ) {
            if (now - it->second.timestamp >= cache_duration_) {
                it = cache_.erase(it);
            } else {
                ++it;
            }
        }
    }
    
public:
    CachingDecorator(std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> processor,
                     std::chrono::seconds duration = std::chrono::seconds(300))
        : PaymentProcessorDecorator(std::move(processor)),
          cache_duration_(duration) {}
    
    PaymentResult process(const PaymentRequest& request) override {
        std::string cache_key = request.order_id;
        
        std::cout << "    [CachingDecorator] Checking cache for order: " << cache_key << "\n";
        
        if (auto it = cache_.find(cache_key); it != cache_.end()) {
            auto age = std::chrono::steady_clock::now() - it->second.timestamp;
            
            if (age < cache_duration_) {
                std::cout << "    [CachingDecorator] Cache HIT! Returning cached result\n";
                return it->second.result;
            }
            std::cout << "    [CachingDecorator] Cache entry expired, removing\n";
            cache_.erase(it);
        }
        
        std::cout << "    [CachingDecorator] Cache MISS, processing...\n";
        PaymentResult result = PaymentProcessorDecorator::process(request);
        
        if (result.success) {
            cache_[cache_key] = {std::chrono::steady_clock::now(), result};
            std::cout << "    [CachingDecorator] Result cached\n";
            
            if (cache_.size() > max_entries_) {
                evict_expired();
            }
        }
        
        return result;
    }
    
    std::string processor_name() const override {
        return "Cached(" + wrapped_->processor_name() + ")";
    }
};

// ============================================================================
// PATTERN 2: FACTORY METHOD - For creating configured processors
// ============================================================================

enum class ProcessorTier {
    Basic,
    Standard,    // Logging + Validation
    Premium,     // Logging + Validation + Retry
    Enterprise   // All decorators + Caching
};

class PaymentProcessorFactory {
public:
    static std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> create(ProcessorTier tier) {
        switch (tier) {
            case ProcessorTier::Basic:
                return std::make_unique<BasicPaymentProcessor>();
                
            case ProcessorTier::Standard:
                return wrap_with_validation(
                    std::make_unique<LoggingDecorator>(
                        std::make_unique<BasicPaymentProcessor>()
                    )
                );
                
            case ProcessorTier::Premium:
                return std::make_unique<LoggingDecorator>(
                    std::make_unique<RetryDecorator>(
                        wrap_with_validation(
                            std::make_unique<BasicPaymentProcessor>()
                        ),
                        3, 50
                    )
                );
                
            case ProcessorTier::Enterprise:
                return std::make_unique<CachingDecorator>(
                    std::make_unique<LoggingDecorator>(
                        std::make_unique<RetryDecorator>(
                            wrap_with_validation(
                                std::make_unique<BasicPaymentProcessor>()
                            ),
                            3, 50
                        )
                    )
                );
        }
        return nullptr;
    }
    
    // Builder pattern for custom configurations
    class Builder {
    private:
        std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> base_;
        
    public:
        Builder(std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> base) : base_(std::move(base)) {}
        
        Builder& with_validation() {
            base_ = wrap_with_validation(std::move(base_));
            return *this;
        }
        
        Builder& with_logging() {
            base_ = std::make_unique<LoggingDecorator>(std::move(base_));
            return *this;
        }
        
        Builder& with_retry(int max_retries = 3, int delay_ms = 100) {
            base_ = std::make_unique<RetryDecorator>(std::move(base_), max_retries, delay_ms);
            return *this;
        }
        
        Builder& with_caching(std::chrono::seconds duration = std::chrono::seconds(300)) {
            base_ = std::make_unique<CachingDecorator>(std::move(base_), duration);
            return *this;
        }
        
        std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> build() {
            return std::move(base_);
        }
    };
    
private:
    static std::unique_ptr<ValidationDecorator> wrap_with_validation(
        std::unique_ptr<IPaymentProcessor> wrapped) {
        
        auto validator = std::make_unique<ValidationDecorator>(std::move(wrapped));
        validator->add_validator(std::make_unique<AmountValidation>());
        validator->add_validator(std::make_unique<CurrencyValidation>());
        return validator;
    }
};

// ============================================================================
// DEMONSTRATION
// ============================================================================

void print_separator(const std::string& title) {
    std::cout << "\n";
    std::cout << "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n";
    std::cout << "  " << title << "\n";
    std::cout << "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n";
}

int main() {
    std::cout << "PAYMENT PROCESSOR - DECORATOR PATTERN DEMONSTRATION\n";
    
    // Test Case 1: Invalid payment (will be caught by validation)
    {
        print_separator("TEST 1: Validation Decorator in Action");
        
        auto processor = PaymentProcessorFactory::Builder(
            std::make_unique<BasicPaymentProcessor>()
        )
        .with_validation()
        .build();
        
        std::cout << "Processor: " << processor->processor_name() << "\n";
        
        PaymentRequest invalid_request{
            .order_id = "ORD-001",
            .customer_id = "CUST-100",
            .amount = -50.0,  // Invalid!
            .currency = "USD",
            .payment_method = "credit_card"
        };
        
        std::cout << "Processing: " << invalid_request.to_string() << "\n";
        auto result = processor->process(invalid_request);
        std::cout << "Final Result: " << (result.success ? "SUCCESS" : "FAILED") << "\n";
    }
    
    // Test Case 2: Full decorator stack with retry
    {
        print_separator("TEST 2: Full Decorator Stack (Validation + Logging + Retry)");
        
        auto processor = PaymentProcessorFactory::Builder(
            std::make_unique<BasicPaymentProcessor>()
        )
        .with_validation()
        .with_logging()
        .with_retry(3, 50)
        .build();
        
        std::cout << "Processor: " << processor->processor_name() << "\n\n";
        
        PaymentRequest request{
            .order_id = "ORD-002",
            .customer_id = "CUST-200",
            .amount = 150.00,
            .currency = "EUR",
            .payment_method = "paypal"
        };
        
        std::cout << "Processing: " << request.to_string() << "\n";
        auto result = processor->process(request);
        std::cout << "Final Result: " << (result.success ? "SUCCESS" : "FAILED") 
                  << " | TX: " << result.transaction_id << "\n";
    }
    
    // Test Case 3: Caching in action (same order processed twice)
    {
        print_separator("TEST 3: Caching Decorator (Idempotency)");
        
        auto processor = PaymentProcessorFactory::Builder(
            std::make_unique<BasicPaymentProcessor>()
        )
        .with_caching()
        .with_logging()
        .build();
        
        std::cout << "Processor: " << processor->processor_name() << "\n\n";
        
        PaymentRequest request{
            .order_id = "ORD-003",  // Same order ID
            .customer_id = "CUST-300",
            .amount = 999.99,
            .currency = "GBP",
            .payment_method = "bank_transfer"
        };
        
        std::cout << "--- First Request ---\n";
        auto result1 = processor->process(request);
        std::cout << "Result: " << result1.transaction_id << "\n\n";
        
        std::cout << "--- Second Request (same order ID) ---\n";
        auto result2 = processor->process(request);
        std::cout << "Result: " << result2.transaction_id << "\n";
    }
    
    // Test Case 4: Compare different factory tiers

import os

except ImportError:

# CIFAR-10 Paths
CIFAR10_DATA_DIR = '/data/defense/cifar10/data'

# Fashion-MNIST Paths

FMNIST_MODEL_DIR = '/models/defense/fmnist'

FMNIST_DEFENSE_DIR = '/output/defense/fmnist'

# ADP Network Parameters
